run_context: Decider run: Processing WIN-021.c2.json (Cycle 2 review). 3 issues created (ISS-533/534/535). 3 patches written.

patches.rationale: WIN-021 code_analysis.monitoring is tagged 'hardcoded' but monitor.py has no gyroscopic precession domain — our own detail_evidence confirms this. 'Hardcoded' means monitor.py has a static pred==obs check; 'none' means monitor.py doesn't touch this WIN at all. The wrong tag inflates the 'monitoring illusion' count in Part 4.6 statistics. Curmudgeon Cycle 2 confirmed: no precession domain exists in the 39-domain audit engine.
patches.apply_note: Use apply-patches.js which handles the parsed JSON field. The code_analysis object for WIN-021 needs monitoring changed from 'hardcoded' to 'none'. Alternatively: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),w=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('data/wins.json','utf8'));w.find(x=>x.id==='021').code_analysis.monitoring='none';fs.writeFileSync('data/wins.json',JSON.stringify(w,null,2));console.log('done')"

patches.find: Units error: dome gives rad/s² (acceleration) not rad/s (velocity). Rate derived circularly from assumed dome parameters
patches.replace: Units error (rad/s² vs rad/s) and 37% value mismatch; no derivation connects dome geometry to the claimed number
patches.rationale: The summary finding currently emphasizes only the units error. Adding the 37% numerical discrepancy makes the summary table entry more impactful: readers see both independent failures (wrong units AND wrong value) without having to click through to the detail. The curmudgeon Cycle 2 review specifically recommended this change: 'consider expanding the finding to include 37% discrepancy.'

patches.replace: in its multi-domain automated audit engine
patches.rationale: The phrase '39-domain' is a hardcoded count for monitor.py's audit engine. If the dome model adds or removes monitoring domains in a future version, this number becomes stale. Softening to 'multi-domain' preserves the key point (precession is not monitored at all) without a specific count that can drift. Minor robustness improvement.
